'''Fadden''' was a [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Gamer2" /> located in the [[Fadden system/Legends|Fadden system]], a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="TEA Fadden" /> It was settled by [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] of the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="Gamer2" /> which was native to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] planet [[Duro/Legends|Duro]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

Fadden was situated in the territory of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] around [[22,000 BBY/Legends|22,000 BBY]]. By that [[Time/Legends|time]], it was connected by a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies']] [[Commenor system/Legends|Commenor system]]. The [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route had been extended to the [[Pyrshak system]] by [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] but had ceased to exist by the time of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] of [[3976 BBY]]–[[3960 BBY]].<ref name="TEA Fadden" />

==Behind the scenes==
Fadden was first mentioned in "[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Duros]]," an article authored by [[Bart Armstrong]] and [[Steve Miller]] with [[Cory Herndon]] and published in [[Star Wars Gamer 2|''Star Wars Gamer'' 2]]<ref name="Gamer2" /> in [[February]] [[2001]].<ref name="Gamer2 date">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010118.html|text=''Star Wars Gamer'' #2}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Fadden [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square N-11.<ref name="TEA Fadden" />
